Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Why opportunity cost exists as decisions are made to allocate limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants
Elaboration: Explaining the concept of “economic scarcity” in relation to “What to produce?”, “How to produce?” or “For whom to produce?”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Why opportunity cost exists as decisions are made to allocate limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants
Elaboration: Identifying the needs and wants of a local community, and exploring how limited resources are currently used to meet unlimited needs and wants

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Why opportunity cost exists as decisions are made to allocate limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants
Elaboration: Explaining how economic resources might be used more sustainably to meet needs and wants into the future

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Why opportunity cost exists as decisions are made to allocate limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants
Elaboration: Investigating how First Nations communities use exchange systems (barter) or networks (partnerships) to decide about the use of limited resources in sustainable ways

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Why opportunity cost exists as decisions are made to allocate limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants
Elaboration: Explaining the concept of “opportunity cost” in relation to a choice; for example, if a student chooses to spend their time (resource) riding their bike after school, they cannot go for a swim (trade-off)

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons businesses exist and how different types of businesses provide goods and services
Elaboration: Identifying why businesses exist; for example, to produce goods and services, to make a profit, to provide employment, and investigating the different ways that goods and services are provided to people, such as through shopping centres, local markets, online, small independent stores and remote community stores

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons businesses exist and how different types of businesses provide goods and services
Elaboration: Identifying the features of different types of businesses, including sole proprietorship, partnership, cooperative, corporation, franchise and not-for-profit organisation

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons businesses exist and how different types of businesses provide goods and services
Elaboration: Investigating a business owned by First Nations Australians, exploring the types of goods and services offered and why this business was started

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons businesses exist and how different types of businesses provide goods and services
Elaboration: Identifying examples of different types of businesses that sell goods and/or services in the local community; for example, a sole proprietor operating a car yard, a partnership providing legal services or a corporation selling groceries through a chain of supermarkets

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Characteristics of entrepreneurs and how these influence the success of a business
Elaboration: Identifying examples of entrepreneurs and the entrepreneurial knowledge and skills that they bring to their business, such as recognising opportunities, establishing a shared vision and making decisions about how the business is run

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Characteristics of entrepreneurs and how these influence the success of a business
Elaboration: Describing the characteristics of First Nations Australian entrepreneurs; for example, community-run companies such as the Gumatj Aboriginal Corporation and individuals such as Dean Foley and Tahnee Edwards, by asking questions such as “What is their story?”, “How did they achieve success?” and “What processes did they apply?”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Characteristics of entrepreneurs and how these influence the success of a business
Elaboration: Analysing the influence of values on entrepreneurial decision-making; for example, identifying and taking advantage of an opportunity, negotiating with stakeholders, and complying with the law and regulations

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Characteristics of entrepreneurs and how these influence the success of a business
Elaboration: Combining knowledge, skills and attitudes demonstrated by entrepreneurs with observations of successful local businesses to explain factors that contribute to success, such as seeing and taking advantage of an opportunity or demonstrating initiative and innovation

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons individuals work, the types of work they are involved in, and how they may derive an income
Elaboration: Identifying different types of work; for example, full-time, part-time, casual, at home, paid, unpaid, unrecognised and volunteer, to appreciate the reasons individuals work or contribute to community organisations

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons individuals work, the types of work they are involved in, and how they may derive an income
Elaboration: Identifying the ways people derive an income, such as working for a wage or salary, owning a business, being a shareholder, providing a rental service or receiving a social security benefit

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons individuals work, the types of work they are involved in, and how they may derive an income
Elaboration: Describing examples of how continuity of cultural practices and management of Country/Place contributes to and sustains First Nations communities; for example, regional tourism ventures, coaching and mentoring initiatives, and fostering entrepreneurial and start-up cultures in regional and remote communities

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The reasons individuals work, the types of work they are involved in, and how they may derive an income
Elaboration: Analysing the contribution that work can make to an individual’s identity and role within a community; for example, earning an income, contributing to self-esteem and happiness, supporting the community through volunteering, enhancing material and non-material living standards

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The rights and responsibilities of individuals and businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services
Elaboration: Distinguishing the difference between rights and responsibilities, and creating a list of the rights and responsibilities of individuals and businesses

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The rights and responsibilities of individuals and businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services
Elaboration: Explaining the rights of individuals and businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services; for example, warranties, cooling-off periods and processes for returning goods

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The rights and responsibilities of individuals and businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services
Elaboration: Discussing the responsibilities of businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services, such as the role of mandatory and voluntary standards, and product safety recalls

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: The rights and responsibilities of individuals and businesses in relation to consumer and financial products and services
Elaboration: Explaining the importance of developing personal or business budgets or savings plans before making decisions in relation to consumer and financial products and services

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop questions to investigate a contemporary economic and business issue
Elaboration: Developing questions to form the basis of an investigation; for example, “How does an understanding of consumer rights and responsibilities impact on decision-making?” and “What are the attributes of an entrepreneur?”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop questions to investigate a contemporary economic and business issue
Elaboration: Developing questions, using economic and business concepts and terms; for example, “How do businesses decide what to produce?”, “What is economic scarcity?” and “Why can individuals not have all the items they want, meaning they must choose?”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Locate, select and organise information and data from a range of sources
Elaboration: Locating online sources; for example, using advanced search functions: “define: market” or targeted criteria: “allintext: successful Australian entrepreneurs”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Locate, select and organise information and data from a range of sources
Elaboration: Selecting relevant information and data from sources by asking questions such as “How will the data or information help answer the question?” about websites of government departments or non-government organisations

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Locate, select and organise information and data from a range of sources
Elaboration: Collaborating and safely exchanging information online with an entrepreneur to identify attributes that build success

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Locate, select and organise information and data from a range of sources
Elaboration: Organising data into appropriate formats using specialised digital tools, such as tables and graphs, visual displays

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Interpret information and data to identify economic and business issues, trends and economic cause-and-effect relationships
Elaboration: Interpreting information to identify economic and business issues; for example, inferring the effect of growth in shopping online on the number of customers entering stores

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Interpret information and data to identify economic and business issues, trends and economic cause-and-effect relationships
Elaboration: Interpreting multi-variable data by using interactive digital tools to identify trends and to answer questions such as “For a 10-year period, to what extent has the number of people in casual work changed?”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Interpret information and data to identify economic and business issues, trends and economic cause-and-effect relationships
Elaboration: Interpreting visual displays of multi-variable data to identify a cause-and-effect relationship within an economic and business issue, such as the relationship between income earned by an individual and levels of saving and spending

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop a response to an economic and business issue, identifying potential costs and benefits
Elaboration: Developing a response to an issue by combining known knowledge with new ideas; for example, when purchasing a mobile phone, use the product statement and personal budget to identify benefits and costs

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop a response to an economic and business issue, identifying potential costs and benefits
Elaboration: Identifying and describing reasons that inform the response to an issue; for example, the reasons for using recyclable materials in packaging as a way to support sustainable use of economic resources to meet needs and wants into the future

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop a response to an economic and business issue, identifying potential costs and benefits
Elaboration: Identifying the potential costs and benefits of a decision; for example, deciding whether to work part-time involves identifying benefits such as earning money and recognising costs such as time on the weekend devoted to travelling to and from work

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Develop a response to an economic and business issue, identifying potential costs and benefits
Elaboration: Explaining how making an economic and business decision involves selecting one alternative over another; for example, to satisfy a particular want, a selection of goods or services is preferred or purchased over another

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Create descriptions and explanations, using economic and business knowledge, concepts and terms, and referencing information and data from sources
Elaboration: Developing a response to an issue that orients the audience (for example, peers or community members) to the issue, using relevant economic and business concepts, and terms such as “market”, “income”, “business”, “goods and services” and “costs and benefits”

Subject: Economics and Business
ContentDescription: Create descriptions and explanations, using economic and business knowledge, concepts and terms, and referencing information and data from sources
Elaboration: Explaining ideas or decisions with details and examples, using data and information presented in appropriate formats; for example, visual displays, tables and graphs, or budget and savings plans, and research findings presented in appropriate formats, such as a graphic organiser or summary
